title: Restore core-player sibling playlist loads
summary: 0.6.23 keeps the 0.6.22 rollback base, but restores the pre-0.6.19 core-player file-selection rule so a selected media file rebuilds the truthful sibling playlist again instead of collapsing next, scrub, and movie transport into one-item behavior.
changes: Restore sibling-playlist expansion for supported direct file selections in the shared player media resolver.
Keep image default pause and video default play behavior unchanged on top of that restored playlist truth.
Stamp version 0.6.23 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build a new raw core-player dev_deploy alpha seam and keep any .var output build-only with distributed:false.
reasoning: The later line drifted a hosted-proof policy into the core player by forcing explicit file loads to stay single-item.
The operator's actual core-player workflow expects a selected file to participate in a real sibling playlist so Next and scrub remain meaningful.
The repo already carried the older resolver helper, so the boring fix is to restore that earlier behavior instead of layering another playlist workaround.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Selecting an image or movie file in the core player should rebuild a real playlist again.
Next should stop behaving like a no-op after a normal file selection, and scrub should stop snapping back as if there were only one item.
This slice intentionally changes only the core-player load-policy seam; it is not another TV-proof or sync experiment.
knownIssues: Hosted and preset playlist behavior beyond this core-player recovery should be reintroduced only if the operator asks for them explicitly.
Mixed-media playlist policy across images and videos remains a separate product decision beyond this boring recovery slice.
